body.title: DickSimnel: escalation signal when confidence low or HIGH-inertia hit
body.result: _escalate_ticket() + _should_escalate(); pre/post-inference escalation paths; 9 tests; cc0_dispatch reset to claude worker
body.description:
When DickSimnel's inference returns '## Confidence: low' or encounters a HIGH-inertia file pattern in the ticket description, it should escalate cleanly to CC rather than just declining. The current _decline_ticket() resets the ticket to sprint status with no signal — CC has no way to know Dick looked at this and bounced it.

The escalation path: DickSimnel posts a channel message (DICKSIMNEL_ESCALATE) with its analysis so far, then sets ticket worker to 'claude' and re-queues it to sprint status. CC picks it up with Dick's analysis already attached as a note.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py, devices/granny/dispatch.py

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every escalation at INFO; calm signals (no urgency=high in channel post per memory feedback_calm_signals).

**Scope boundary:** In scope: _escalate_ticket(ticket_id, reason, analysis) method; confidence=low parser from inference response; HIGH-inertia tag detection from ticket tags list; DICKSIMNEL_ESCALATE channel post; worker reset to 'claude'. Out of scope: Granny routing changes, CC inbox integration.

**Completion criteria:** test_dicksimnel_escalate.py passes; _escalate_ticket() posts DICKSIMNEL_ESCALATE to channel, sets worker=claude, resets status=sprint; confidence='low' in inference response triggers escalation path rather than plain decline.
